title: Franz Joseph I of Austria becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Franz Joseph I or Francis Joseph I was Emperor of Austria, King of Hungary, and the ruler of the other states of the Habsburg monarchy from 2 December 1848 until his death in 1916.

title: Maria Theresa of Austria becomes Queen of Bohemia
fact: Maria Theresa was the ruler of the Habsburg monarchy from 1740 until her death in 1780, and the only woman to hold the position in her own right.

title: Charles I of Austria becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Charles I and IV was Emperor of Austria, King of Hungary, and the ruler of the other states of the Habsburg monarchy from November 1916 until the monarchy was abolished in November 1918.

title: Francis II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Francis II and I was the last Holy Roman Emperor as Francis II from 1792 to 1806, and the first Emperor of Austria as Francis I from 1804 to 1835.

title: Sigismund becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Sigismund of Luxembourg was Holy Roman Emperor from 1433 until his death in 1437.

title: Joseph II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Joseph II was Holy Roman Emperor from 18 August 1765 and sole ruler of the Habsburg monarchy from 29 November 1780 until his death.

title: Leopold I, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Leopold I was Holy Roman Emperor, King of Germany, King of Hungary, Croatia, and Bohemia.

title: Ferdinand I, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Ferdinand I was Holy Roman Emperor from 1556, King of Bohemia, Hungary, and Croatia from 1526, and Archduke of Austria from 1521 until his death in 1564.

title: Leopold II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Leopold II was the penultimate Holy Roman Emperor, as well as King of Hungary, Croatia and Bohemia, and Archduke of Austria from 1790 to 1792, and Grand Duke of Tuscany from 1765 to 1790.

title: Ferdinand I of Austria becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Ferdinand I was Emperor of Austria from March 1835 until his abdication in December 1848.

title: Charles IV becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Charles IV was Holy Roman Emperor from 1355 until his death in 1378.

title: Wenceslaus IV of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Wenceslaus IV was King of Bohemia from 1378 until his death and King of Germany from 1376 until he was deposed in 1400.

title: Charles VI, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Charles VI was Holy Roman Emperor and ruler of the Austrian Habsburg monarchy from 1711 until his death, succeeding his elder brother, Joseph I.

title: Matthias Corvinus becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Matthias Corvinus was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1458 to 1490, as Matthias I.

title: Ferdinand II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Ferdinand II was Holy Roman Emperor, King of Bohemia, Hungary, and Croatia from 1619 until his death in 1637.

title: Emperor Rudolf II becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Rudolf II was Holy Roman Emperor (1576–1612), King of Hungary and Croatia, King of Bohemia (1575–1608/1611) and Archduke of Austria (1576–1608).

title: Ferdinand III, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Ferdinand III was Archduke of Austria, King of Hungary and Croatia from 1625, King of Bohemia from 1627 and Holy Roman Emperor from 1637 to his death.

title: Maximilian II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Maximilian II was Holy Roman Emperor from 1564 until his death in 1576.

title: Joseph I, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Joseph I was Holy Roman Emperor and ruler of the Habsburg monarchy from 1705 until his death in 1711.

title: Matthias, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Matthias was Holy Roman Emperor from 1612 to 1619, Archduke of Austria from 1608 to 1619, King of Hungary and Croatia from 1608 to 1618 and King of Bohemia from 1611 to 1617.

title: Albert II of Germany becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Albert the Magnanimous, elected King of the Romans as Albert II, was a member of the House of Habsburg.

title: Ladislaus the Posthumous becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Ladislaus V, more commonly known as Ladislaus the Posthumous, was Duke of Austria and King of Hungary, Croatia and Bohemia.

title: John of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: John of Bohemia, also called the Blind or of Luxembourg, was the Count of Luxembourg from 1313 and King of Bohemia from 1310 and titular King of Poland.

title: Ottokar II of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Ottokar II, the Iron and Golden King, was a member of the Přemyslid dynasty who reigned as King of Bohemia from 1253 until his death in 1278.

title: Frederick V of the Palatinate becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Frederick V was the Elector Palatine of the Rhine in the Holy Roman Empire from 1610 to 1623, and reigned as King of Bohemia from 1619 to 1620.

title: Louis II, King of Hungary, Croatia and Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Louis II was King of Hungary, Croatia and Bohemia from 1516 to 1526.

title: Vladislaus II of Bohemia and Hungary becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Vladislaus II, also known as Vladislav, Władysław or Wladislas ;, was King of Bohemia from 1471 to 1516 and King of Hungary and King of Croatia from 1490 to 1516.

title: Wenceslaus III of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Wenceslaus III was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1301 to 1305, and King of Bohemia and Poland from 1305.

title: Wenceslaus II of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Wenceslaus II Přemyslid was King of Bohemia (1278–1305), Duke of Cracow (1291–1305), and King of Poland (1296–1305).

title: George of Poděbrady becomes King of Bohemia
fact: George of Kunštát and Poděbrady, also known as Poděbrad or Podiebrad, was the sixteenth King of Bohemia, who ruled in 1458–1471.

title: Ottokar I of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Ottokar I was Duke of Bohemia periodically beginning in 1192, then acquired the title of King of Bohemia, first in 1198 from Philip of Swabia, later in 1203 from Otto IV of Brunswick and in 1212 from Frederick II.

title: Wenceslaus I of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Wenceslaus I, called One-Eyed, was King of Bohemia from 1230 to 1253.

title: Henry of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Henry of Gorizia, a member of the House of Gorizia, was Duke of Carinthia and Landgrave of Carniola and Count of Tyrol from 1295 until his death, as well as King of Bohemia, Margrave of Moravia and titular King of Poland in 1306 and again from 1307 until 1310.

title: Vratislaus II of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Vratislaus II, the son of Bretislaus I and Judith of Schweinfurt, was the first King of Bohemia as of 15 June 1085, his royal title granted as a lifetime honorific from Holy Roman Emperor Henry IV that did not establish a hereditary monarchy.

title: Rudolf I of Bohemia becomes King of Bohemia
fact: Rudolf I, also known as Rudolf of Habsburg, was a member of the House of Habsburg, the King of Bohemia and titular King of Poland from 1306 until his death.
